summary: Matched the remaining Yoshi init function in ftYs_Init.c by adding ftYs_Init_8012BECC and helper wrappers/subroutine logic, plus a Yoshi guard motion-vars union member needed to express the function without cross-character/common motion-var abuse. The final diff uses the Yoshi motion-state enum ftYs_MS_GuardOn_0 instead of a raw 0x155 and writes through fp->mv.ys.guard fields rather than pointer-casting unrelated common fields.
what_changed: Added includes for ft/ft_0877.h and ftCommon/ftCo_Guard.h in ftYs_Init.c to support GET_FIGHTER-related access and guard/common helper calls used by the matched function.
Added static no-inline wrapper chain ftYs_Init_8012B8A4_no_inline_2 and ftYs_Init_8012B8A4_no_inline around ftYs_Init_8012B8A4, apparently to preserve call/inlining behavior needed for matching.

Added ftYs_Init_8012BECC_sub, which disables hurt capsules, initializes Yoshi guard motion variables, zeroes a local Vec3, translates a part joint with HSD_JObjSetTranslate, calls the no-inline wrapper, invokes ftCo_80091D58, and plays SFX 0x6E.
Added exported function ftYs_Init_8012BECC, which uses PAD_STACK(8), changes motion state to ftYs_MS_GuardOn_0, calls ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftCo_80092450, and then the new subroutine.
Extended union ftYoshi_MotionVars in ftYoshi/types.h with struct ftYoshi_GuardVars guard, defining fields x0, xA, xD, x10, x14, x18, x1C, and x20 at offsets fp+2340 through fp+2360.
smart_moves: Converted the motion-state argument from a raw 0x155 literal to the fighter motion-state enum ftYs_MS_GuardOn_0, improving readability and helping identify the correct motion-vars union member.
Introduced a Yoshi-specific guard member in ftYoshi_MotionVars, avoiding the earlier pattern of treating unrelated common motion-variable fields as storage for this motion state.
Used explicit no-inline helper wrappers to control generated code shape while still keeping the underlying call to ftYs_Init_8012B8A4 factored.
Kept unknown guard fields conservatively named by offset-style placeholders instead of over-interpreting their semantics.
decomp_lessons: When a function calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with a raw numeric state, resolving it to the fighter-specific motion-state enum can reveal which motion-vars union member is active.

Do not force a match by writing through unrelated union members or pointer-casting floats as integers if the motion state indicates a missing struct variant; add the correct fighter-specific motion-vars member instead.
Small wrapper functions may be useful matching tools when the original codegen requires a particular call boundary or avoided inline expansion.
Stack padding such as PAD_STACK(8) and local struct padding around Vec3 can be part of the match strategy and should be preserved when evidence supports it.
A matched function can expose missing type/header work; here, function matching and motion-var struct definition were linked.
naming_conventions: Unidentified Yoshi init functions retain address-based names such as ftYs_Init_8012BECC and ftYs_Init_8012BECC_sub.
No-inline matching shims were named with a descriptive suffix: ftYs_Init_8012B8A4_no_inline and ftYs_Init_8012B8A4_no_inline_2.
New motion-var struct follows fighter/move naming style: struct ftYoshi_GuardVars inside union ftYoshi_MotionVars as member guard.
Unknown fields in newly introduced structs use offset-like names such as x0, xA, xD, x1C, and x20, with explicit offset comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftYs_Init_8012BECC to match stack layout.
Created a local struct containing u8 _pad[4] followed by Vec3 v before passing &s.v to HSD_JObjSetTranslate, suggesting stack layout/register pressure mattered for matching.
Added a two-level no-inline wrapper chain around ftYs_Init_8012B8A4 to influence call structure and prevent unwanted inlining/codegen differences.
Initialized Vec3 components with a chained assignment to zero before joint translation.
Replaced unsafe-looking casts from the review excerpt with typed fp->mv.ys.guard accesses in the final diff.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: The raw 0x155 value should be a fighter motion-state enum, which should identify the correct union member and probably remove pointer casts that treated floats as s32 values.
review_feedback.outcome: Final diff uses ftYs_MS_GuardOn_0 and adds/accesses fp->mv.ys.guard fields instead of the reviewed pointer-cast/common-field approach.

follow_up_queries: Look for other ftYoshi functions using raw motion-state IDs that could reveal missing ftYoshi_MotionVars union members.
Search for remaining pointer casts into fp->mv fields that may indicate incorrect or missing motion-var struct definitions.
Investigate whether ftYoshi_GuardVars fields x0, xA, xD, x1C, and x20 can be renamed using behavior from guard/egg-shield code.
Check whether the no-inline wrapper pattern appears in nearby matched fighter init functions and can guide future codegen fixes.
Find data-linking work for ftYs_Init.c if later PRs fully link this file, since the author noted data matching was still desired.
